tasks.title: Firestore Favorites Foundation And Rules

Add server rules tests and focused unit coverage for the data contract.

No public counts, tags, manual sort, notifications, recommendations, snapshots, migrations, or dependency changes.
Implementation authorization boundary is edit yes for T001 owned files only; commit no, push no, PR no, merge no, local main sync no.
engineerInstructions: RED: add rules tests for owner access, cross-user denial, update denial, invalid extra fields, and doc id mismatch for both favorite collections.
RED: add service and use-case tests for payload shape, collection mapping, newest-first list loading, favorite id sets, and missing target entries.
GREEN: implement only owned files with repo, service, and use-case boundaries.
Stop if an index, migration, dependency, or wider file edit is required.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-T001.1: Favorite docs are written only under users/{uid}/favoritePosts/{postId} and users/{uid}/favoriteEvents/{eventId}.
AC-T001.2: Favorite docs contain targetId and createdAt only.
AC-T001.3: Add, remove, list, batch status lookup, and latest target resolution use canonical repo, service, and use-case boundaries.
AC-T001.4: Missing target documents are represented without failing the whole favorites page load.
AC-T001.5: Rules tests prove owner-only read, create, delete, update rejection, cross-user denial, extra field denial, and doc id mismatch denial.

evidence.engineerReport: T007 test edits completed in tests/e2e/saved-content-favorites.spec.js and tests/unit/runtime/useMemberFavoritesRuntime.test.jsx. Mapping Engineer modified scripts/test-e2e-branch.sh only in is_emulator_spec() to include saved-content-favorites.spec.js. Focused E2E REST documentExists admin-read cleanup was fixed within the authorized E2E file by adding Authorization: Bearer owner. Post-T007 permission console bugfix handled the user report where after login /posts and /events showed FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions because localhost:3001 connects to production Firestore and background favorite status hydration reads users/{uid}/favoritePosts|favoriteEvents/{targetId}; if production rules do not yet include new favorite subcollection rules, Firestore returns permission-denied. The fix treats only permission-denied during background favorite status hydration as empty/no-op fallback, while generic errors still throw/log and user-triggered add/remove favorite failures still rollback and show toast.
evidence.reviewerReport: review_passed for T007 test content, scripts/test-e2e-branch.sh branch-gate mapping, and E2E REST auth cleanup. Post-T007 permission console bugfix reviewer decision: review_passed; no blocking findings. No known verification blockers remain. Residual risk: until production Firestore rules are deployed, initial background favorite state may show unfilled/empty, but console/page break is avoided; active user write failures still toast. Closeout remains unauthorized: commit no, push no, PR no, merge no, CI-watch no, local-main-sync no.
